steps.title: Identify the grammatical role needed
steps.content: The subject “people in the Americas” (preceded by the temporal adverbial “for thousands of years”) requires a main verb to follow. The blank must contain a finite verb form capable of serving as the main verb of the independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the finite verb requirement
steps.content:
An independent clause requires a finite verb as its main verb. “Have used” (B) is the present perfect tense—a finite form expressing an action repeated or continuous over a time span extending from past to present, the standard form after “for thousands of years.” All other choices are non-finite forms (infinitive and participles) that cannot function as a main verb.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
